verse_specific_anchors: sat / asat (the verse's literal contrast — preserved by the analog's 'has being' / 'has no being' framing)
tattva-darśin (preserved verbatim and named as the operational role)
dṛṣṭa antaḥ (the seers' settled-conclusion move, mirrored by the analog's 'with sources' requirement)
ubhayoḥ api (of both — the analog explicitly tracks both the phantom bug and the unobserved real need)
verdict_reason: I tested the analog against neighboring verses. BG 2.17 names the substrate as pervasive imperishable — but the analog here is about cutting between real-and-unreal at the diagnostic level, not about pervasiveness; 2.17's analog is the protocol/contract, which is a different operational move. BG 2.18 licenses action — the analog here is not about action but about discrimination. BG 2.19/2.20 negate agency — the analog here is about ontological cut. Outside Block B: BG 18.66's release-of-dharma is a different move entirely. The 'sat/asat ontological cut' is verse-specific and the engineering analog (real-need vs phantom-bug) anchors on it correctly. PASS.
